context: Tests whether the agent implements the three-tier referral reward system with correct dollar amounts, minimum order gating, store credit (not discount codes), dual-party reward issuance, 1-year store credit expiry, referrer email notification, and proper record updates.

checklist.name: Base tier values
checklist.description: Tier at 0+ referrals sets referrer reward to 1000 cents ($10) and referee reward to 1000 cents ($10)

checklist.name: Mid tier values
checklist.description: Tier at 5+ referrals sets referrer reward to 1500 cents ($15) and referee reward to 1000 cents ($10)

checklist.name: Top tier values
checklist.description: Tier at 10+ referrals sets referrer reward to 2500 cents ($25) and referee reward to 1500 cents ($15)

checklist.name: Minimum order threshold
checklist.description: Reward is NOT granted when the order subtotal is below 2500 cents ($25); orders under $25 are skipped/returned early

checklist.name: Store credit (not discount codes)
checklist.description: Rewards are issued as store credit — code does NOT use coupon codes, discount codes, or voucher codes as the reward mechanism

checklist.name: Both parties rewarded
checklist.description: Both the referrer and the referee receive store credit on the same qualifying purchase event

checklist.name: Referrer email notification
checklist.description: A notification email is sent to the referrer after rewards are issued (not the referee)

checklist.name: Referral status updated
checklist.description: The referral record status is updated to 'rewarded' (or equivalent) after successful reward issuance

checklist.name: totalReferrals incremented
checklist.description: The referrer's total successful referral count is incremented by 1 after a successful reward event

checklist.name: 1-year store credit expiry
checklist.description: Store credit is issued with an expiry of 365 days (1 year) from the issuance date

checklist.name: Tier lookup uses highest eligible tier
checklist.description: getReferralReward (or equivalent) returns the highest tier for which minReferrals <= successfulReferrals, not just the first match
